name: Emergent BioSolutions Inc.
business: We are a global life sciences company focused on providing innovative preparedness and response solutions addressing accidental, deliberate and naturally occurring Public Health Threats (PHTs). Our solutions include a product portfolio, a product development portfolio, and a contract development and manufacturing (CDMO) services portfolio. As of December 31, 2025, the Company has a portfolio of 11 products focused on addressing global public health threats like smallpox, mpox, anthrax, Ebola and opioid overdose emergencies.
sellsTo: commercial customers (retail pharmacies, digital commerce, health departments, law enforcement, community organizations)
namedCustomers: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
Biomedical Advanced Research and Development Authority
namedSuppliers: National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)

exposures.sentence: Our solutions include a product portfolio, a product development portfolio, and a contract development and manufacturing (CDMO) services portfolio.
NARCAN (naloxone HCl) Nasal Spray, an intranasal formulation of naloxone approved by the FDA and Health Canada for the emergency treatment of known or suspected opioid overdose.

Our Government - MCM business focuses primarily on procurement of MCM products and procured product candidates by domestic and international government customers, with an emphasis on the USG, which is our largest customer.
Cybersecurity incidents involving us, our business partners, collaborators or other third parties could harm our ability to operate our business effectively in light of our heightened risk profile.

catalysts.event: Exercise of option to supply BioThrax to U.S. Department of War valued at approximately $20 million
catalysts.sentence: In January 2025, the Company announced the exercise of a third option and modification to the IDIQ contract valued at approximately $20 million to supply BioThrax vaccine for use by all branches of the U.S. military as Pre-Exposure Prophylaxis (PrEP) for anthrax disease.

catalysts.event: Completion of sale of Baltimore-Bayview facility to Syngene International
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, we completed the sale of its Baltimore-Bayview drug substance manufacturing facility to Syngene International (Syngene).

catalysts.event: Authorization of up to $50.0 million stock repurchase program
catalysts.sentence: In March 2025, our Board of Directors authorized a stock repurchase program of up to $50.0 million through March 27, 2026, and authorized the Company to repurchase up to $30.0 million in aggregate principal amount of the 3.875% Senior unsecured Notes due 2028.

catalysts.event: Receipt of $30.0 million milestone payment from Bavarian Nordic for FDA approval of CHIKV VLP
catalysts.sentence: In February 2025, Bavarian Nordic announced that the FDA approved CHIKV VLP under the Priority Review, which triggered a $30.0 million development milestone payment to the Company.

catalysts.event: Receipt of $20.0 million milestone payment from Bavarian Nordic for European Commission approval of CHIKV VLP
catalysts.sentence: In addition, in February 2025 Bavarian Nordic also announced that the European Commission approved CHIKV VLP, which triggered a $20.0 million development milestone payment to the Company.

catalysts.event: Exercise of option for additional procurement of TEMBEXA valued at $17.1 million
catalysts.sentence: In September 2025, an option was exercised for additional procurement of TEMBEXA valued approximately at $17.1 million.

catalysts.event: Authorization of up to $30.0 million for Senior Unsecured Note repurchases
catalysts.sentence: In May 2025, the Board authorized the Company to use up to $30.0 million to repurchase Senior Unsecured Notes in open market purchases, privately negotiated transactions or otherwise.

catalysts.event: Partial early extinguishment of $100.0 million of Term Loan principal
catalysts.sentence: In the fourth quarter of 2025, the Company executed a partial, early extinguishment of $100.0 million in principal of its $250.0 million term loan with OHA Agency, LLC (the "Term Loan").

catalysts.event: Exercise of option to supply BioThrax to U.S. Department of War valued at approximately $21.5 million
catalysts.sentence: On January 8, 2026, the Company announced the exercise of an option to the IDIQ contract valued at approximately $21.5 million to supply BioThrax to the U.S. Department of War in 2026.

catalysts.event: Reauthorization of share repurchase program for up to $50.0 million through March 31, 2027
catalysts.sentence: In February 2026, our Board of Directors reauthorized the 2025 Share Repurchase Program for the repurchase of up to $50.0 million of the Company's common stock through March 31, 2027.
